identificationModule.briefTitle: Fractional Flow Reserve to Determine Atherosclerosis Renovascular Hypertension Stenting
identificationModule.officialTitle: Fractional Flow Reserve to Determine the ApproprIateness of Percutaneous Renal Artery Intervention in Atherosclerosis Renovascular Hypertension Patients: a Pilot Randomized Trial

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
Although randomized trials have demonstrated there is no benefit of renal-artery stenting in addition to medical therapy for patients with atherosclerosis renal artery stenosis, many patients indeed gained benefit in daily practices after stenting, such as reduction in blood pressure and recovery in renal functions. One important gap is that there is no universal standard to determine whether to stent in these patients. Fraction Flow Reserve (FFR) has been studied for many year in chronic coronary heart disease and FFR-guided revascularization strategy is known to be better than both angiography-guided revascularization and medication alone. The goal of this clinical trial is to learn whether Fraction Flow Reserve (FFR) is appropriate to determine stenting in hypertension patients with atherosclerosis renal artery stenosis. The main questions it aims to answer are:

* Is it appropriate to use FFR to determine whether or not stenting for hypertension patients with atherosclerosis renal artery stenosis?
* To provide detailed data supporting design of further trial, such as sample size calculating, cut-off value for FFR in renal artery stenosis, etc.

Participants met the inclusive/exclusive criteria will be randomized to stenting or not in the renal artery, then hyperemic FFR induced by dopamine will be measured in all participants. If FFR is ≥0.80, randomization will be applied. If FFR is \<0.80, randomization will be ignored, and stenting will be performed as planned. The blood pressure and anti-hypertensive medications will be compared before and 3 months after the procedure based on ambulatory blood pressure monitoring, all participants will be followed up for 1 year.

armGroups.label: Not stenting
armGroups.description: Hyperemic FFR induced by 50μg/kg of dopamine via renal artery will be measured. If FFR is ≥0.80, randomization will be applied, and no stenting will be implanted. If FFR is \<0.80, randomization will be ignored, and stenting will be performed.
interventionNames: Diagnostic Test: Fractional Flow Reserve, Renal

armGroups.label: Stenting
armGroups.description: Hyperemic FFR induced by 50μg/kg of dopamine via renal artery will be measured. No matter FFR is, stenting will be performed as planned.
interventionNames: Diagnostic Test: Fractional Flow Reserve, Renal

interventions.name: Dopamine
interventions.description: A bolus dose of 50μg/kg dopamine via renal artery to induce hyperemic status

interventions.name: Fractional Flow Reserve, Renal
interventions.description: Renal FFR will be measured based on SOP

interventions.name: Renal artery stenting
interventions.description: Renal artery stenting will be implanted based on the protocol

primaryOutcomes.measure: Change in daytime mean systolic blood pressure as measured by 24-hour Ambulatory Blood Pressure Monitoring (ABPM)
Change in the composite index of antihypertensive drugs
primaryOutcomes.description: Change in the composite index of antihypertensive drugs. Drug Composite Index = Weight (number of classes of antihypertensive drugs) × (sum of doses)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in systolic blood pressure as measured by 24-hour ABPM
Change in diastolic blood pressure as measured by 24-hour ABPM
Change in the composite index of antihypertensive drugs to reach target blood pressure
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in the composite index of antihypertensive drugs to reach target blood pressure. Drug Composite Index = Weight (number of classes of antihypertensive drugs) × (sum of doses)

secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From baseline to 6 months, 1 year post-procedure

secondaryOutcomes.description: Based on universal definition of acute myocardial infarction
Based on medical records under outcome committee's judge

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Rehospitalization due to heart failure incidence
secondaryOutcomes.description: Based on medical records under outcome committee's judge

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Increase in serum creatinine or dialysis
